lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: Here’s a knocking indeed! If a man were porter of hell-gate, he

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: should have old turning the key. Knock, knock, knock! Who’s there, i’

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: the name of Beelzebub? Here’s a farmer, that hanged himself on the

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: expectation of plenty: come in time; have napkins enow about you; here

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: you’ll sweat for’t. Knock, knock! Who’s there, in the other devil’s name?

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: Faith, here’s an equivocator, that could swear in both the scales against

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: either scale; who committed treason enough for God’s sake, yet could not

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: equivocate to heaven: O, come in, equivocator. Knock, knock, knock! Who’s there?

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: Faith, here’s an English tailor come hither, for stealing out of a

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: French hose: come in, tailor; here you may roast your goose. Knock,

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: knock; never at quiet! What are you? But this place is too

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: cold for hell. I’ll devil-porter it no further: I had thought to

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: have let in some of all professions that go the primrose way

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: to the everlasting bonfire. Anon, anon! I pray you, remember the porter.

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: Opens the gate.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Was it so late, friend, ere you went to bed,

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: That you do lie so late?

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: ’Faith sir, we were carousing till the second cock: and drink, sir,

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: is a great provoker of three things.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: What three things does drink especially provoke?

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: Marry, sir, nose-painting, sleep, and urine. Lechery, sir, it provokes, and unprovokes;

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: it provokes the desire, but it takes away the performance: therefore, much

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: drink may be said to be an equivocator with lechery: it makes

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: him, and it mars him; it sets him on, and it takes

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: him off; it persuades him, and disheartens him; makes him stand to,

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: and not stand to; in conclusion, equivocates him in a sleep, and,

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: giving him the lie, leaves him.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: I believe drink gave thee the lie last night.

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: That it did, sir, i’ the very throat on me: but I

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: requited him for his lie; and, I think, being too strong for

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: him, though he took up my legs sometime, yet I made a

lines.speaker: PORTER
lines.text: shift to cast him.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Is thy master stirring?

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Good morrow, noble sir.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Good morrow, both.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Is the king stirring, worthy thane?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: He did command me to call timely on him:

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: I have almost slipp’d the hour.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I’ll bring you to him.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: I know this is a joyful trouble to you;

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: But yet ’tis one.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The labour we delight in physics pain.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: This is the door.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: I’ll make so bold to call,

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: For ’tis my limited service.

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Goes the king hence to-day?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: He does: he did appoint so.

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: The night has been unruly: where we lay,

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Our chimneys were blown down; and, as they say,

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Lamentings heard i’ the air; strange screams of death,

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: And prophesying with accents terrible

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Of dire combustion and confused events

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: New hatch’d to the woeful time: the obscure bird

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Clamour’d the livelong night: some say, the earth

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Was feverous and did shake.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: ’Twas a rough night.

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: My young remembrance cannot parallel

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: A fellow to it.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: O horror, horror, horror! Tongue nor heart

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Cannot conceive nor name thee!

lines.speaker: MACBETH LENNOX
lines.text: What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Confusion now hath made his masterpiece!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Most sacrilegious murder hath broke ope

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: The Lord’s anointed temple, and stole thence

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: The life o’ the building!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: What is’t you say? the life?

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Mean you his majesty?

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Approach the chamber, and destroy your sight

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: With a new Gorgon: do not bid me speak;

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: See, and then speak yourselves. Awake, awake!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Ring the alarum-bell. Murder and treason!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Banquo and Donalbain! Malcolm! awake!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Shake off this downy sleep, death’s counterfeit,

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: And look on death itself! up, up, and see

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: The great doom’s image! Malcolm! Banquo!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: As from your graves rise up, and walk like sprites,

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: To countenance this horror! Ring the bell.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: What’s the business,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: That such a hideous trumpet calls to parley

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: The sleepers of the house? speak, speak!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: O gentle lady,

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: ’Tis not for you to hear what I can speak:

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: The repetition, in a woman’s ear,

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Would murder as it fell.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Woe, alas!

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: What, in our house?

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Too cruel any where.

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Dear Duff, I prithee, contradict thyself,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: And say it is not so.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Had I but died an hour before this chance,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I had lived a blessed time; for, from this instant,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: There’s nothing serious in mortality:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: All is but toys: renown and grace is dead;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The wine of life is drawn, and the mere lees

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Is left this vault to brag of.

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: What is amiss?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: You are, and do not know’t:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The spring, the head, the fountain of your blood

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Is stopp’d; the very source of it is stopp’d.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Your royal father’s murder’d.

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: O, by whom?

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Those of his chamber, as it seem’d, had done’t:

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Their hands and faces were all badged with blood;

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: So were their daggers, which unwiped we found

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Upon their pillows:

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: They stared, and were distracted; no man’s life

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Was to be trusted with them.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: O, yet I do repent me of my fury,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That I did kill them.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Wherefore did you so?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Who can be wise, amazed, temperate and furious,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Loyal and neutral, in a moment? No man:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The expedition my violent love

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Outrun the pauser, reason. Here lay Duncan,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: His silver skin laced with his golden blood;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And his gash’d stabs look’d like a breach in nature

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: For ruin’s wasteful entrance: there, the murderers,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Steep’d in the colours of their trade, their daggers

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Unmannerly breech’d with gore: who could refrain,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That had a heart to love, and in that heart

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Courage to make’s love known?

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Help me hence, ho!

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: Look to the lady.

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Why do we hold our tongues,

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: That most may claim this argument for ours?

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: What should be spoken here, where our fate,

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: Hid in an auger-hole, may rush, and seize us?

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: Let’s away;

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: Our tears are not yet brew’d.

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Nor our strong sorrow

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Upon the foot of motion.

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Look to the lady: Lady Macbeth is carried out.

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: And when we have our naked frailties hid,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: That suffer in exposure, let us meet,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: And question this most bloody piece of work,

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: To know it further. Fears and scruples shake us:

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: In the great hand of God I stand; and thence

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Against the undivulged pretence I fight

lines.speaker: BANQUO
lines.text: Of treasonous malice.

lines.speaker: MACDUFF
lines.text: And so do I.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: So all.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Let’s briefly put on manly readiness,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And meet i’ the hall together.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Well contented.

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: What will you do? Let’s not consort with them:

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: To show an unfelt sorrow is an office

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Which the false man does easy. I’ll to England.

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: To Ireland, I; our separated fortune

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: Shall keep us both the safer: where we are,

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: There’s daggers in men’s smiles: the near in blood,

lines.speaker: DONALBAIN
lines.text: The nearer bloody.

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: This murderous shaft that’s shot

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Hath not yet lighted, and our safest way

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Is to avoid the aim. Therefore, to horse;

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: And let us not be dainty of leave-taking,

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: But shift away: there’s warrant in that theft

lines.speaker: MALCOLM
lines.text: Which steals itself, when there’s no mercy left.

stage_directions.text: Our knocking has awaked him; here he comes.
O Banquo, Banquo, Our royal master’s murder’d!
Reenter Macbeth and Lennox, with Ross.
Exeunt all but Malcolm and Donalbain.

glosses.definition: the Porter's famous opening of the comic relief scene after the murder
the Porter imagines himself as the gatekeeper of hell — casting Macbeth's castle as a literal infernal site
chief of demons; a name for Satan
enough handkerchiefs (to wipe the hell-sweat)

a liar by mental reservation — direct allusion to the Jesuit Henry Garnet, executed 1606 for the Gunpowder Plot; his 'Treatise of Equivocation' taught that swearing to God while reserving mental truths was not technically perjury. Shakespeare's audience would have known the reference
the second cockcrow, around 3 a.m.
drink, which provokes (1) nose-painting, (2) sleep, (3) urine; and (4) lechery
redness of the nose from drunkenness
puts him to sleep before he can consummate — an equivocation against lechery
tripped you up; knocked you down
in good time; early
appointed duty
cries in the air — supernatural omens
the owl, bird of darkness
shouted all night long
feverish; shaking
Macduff's cry upon finding Duncan — the triple repetition echoes the Weird Sisters' tripling
the king's body as a consecrated holy place — regicide as sacrilege
a new Medusa — a sight that turns viewers to stone

glosses.phrase: Shake off this downy sleep, death’s counterfeit
glosses.definition: wake up; sleep is the forgery of death
a preview of the Last Judgment
covered up to the haft with blood — the daggers Macbeth plants on the grooms
a tiny crack; a place too small to hide in — Donalbain's image for the danger all around
close kin to Duncan (and thus in danger)
Donalbain's wisdom: trust no man's face

annotations.title: The equivocator and the Gunpowder Plot
annotations.body:
The Porter imagines himself as the gatekeeper of hell and lets in a procession of the damned. The second damned soul is an 'equivocator, that could swear in both the scales against either scale.' For a London audience in 1606, the reference was unmissable.

Henry Garnet, the Jesuit superior in England, had been executed on May 3, 1606 — months before the play's earliest likely performance — for his role in the Gunpowder Plot (November 1605). His *Treatise of Equivocation*, circulated in manuscript, argued that a Catholic under interrogation could swear 'truly' while mentally reserving crucial qualifications. The doctrine was held up at his trial as proof that Catholics could not be trusted to swear loyalty. Garnet was the equivocator in public imagination.

The Porter is not engaged in incidental humor. He is placing Duncan's murder — the regicide happening upstairs — in direct conversation with the Gunpowder Plot, the regicide that nearly happened a few months earlier. Both are attacks on a Scottish king on English soil. Both involved subjects sworn to protect the king and the oaths they broke. The play's audience heard the political analogy; James I heard it too, and would have approved.

When Macbeth himself invokes 'the equivocation of the fiend' at 5.5.42, he is naming the same doctrine — but now against his own ruin.
sources: Garry Wills, *Witches and Jesuits: Shakespeare's Macbeth* (1995)

annotations.title: The discovery of Duncan's murder
annotations.body:
Macduff finds Duncan. His reaction — 'O horror, horror, horror! / Tongue nor heart cannot conceive nor name thee' — gives the play its iconic scream. He returns with the body-image: 'Confusion now hath made his masterpiece! / Most sacrilegious murder hath broke ope / The Lord's anointed temple.' Duncan is figured as a holy building, regicide as architectural sacrilege.

Note who speaks and who doesn't. Macduff cries out; Banquo swears an oath of service ('In the great hand of God I stand'); Malcolm and Donalbain whisper asides about fleeing. Macbeth delivers a prepared speech so over-rhetorical that a suspicious listener would recognize it as performance — 'Had I but died an hour before this chance, / I had lived a blessed time.' Lady Macbeth is ostentatiously shocked, and faints at the right moment.

The scene is an audition for every character's reaction to violence, and the play rewards the ones who are honestly horrified. Macduff will become the avenger. Malcolm and Donalbain's decision to flee separately, to England and Ireland, is the political act that will eventually defeat Macbeth.

trials.prompt: The Porter's 'equivocator' is a direct allusion to:
options: Henry Garnet, the Jesuit executed May 1606 for the Gunpowder Plot — his 'Treatise of Equivocation' justified sworn mental reservation

trials.prompt: The Porter scene is not filler. Its function is:
options: To place Duncan's murder in direct dialogue with the Gunpowder Plot — another regicide that nearly happened

trials.prompt: Macbeth's prepared speech on finding Duncan ('Had I but died an hour before this chance') is:
options: Over-rhetorical performance that a suspicious listener would spot as staged
Flee separately to England and Ireland — the political decision that preserves the legitimate line

trials.prompt: Lennox's report of the night — tempests, crying in the air, the owl's scream — functions as:
options: Portents — the natural order registering the regicide before it is announced
